title: hooks: Add pre-create hooks for runtime-config manipulation
body:
There's been a lot of discussion over in opencontainers/runc#1811 about how to support the NVIDIA folks and others who want to be able to create devices (possibly after having loaded kernel modules) and bind userspace libraries into the container.  Currently that's happening in the middle of `runc`'s create-time mount handling before the container pivots to its new root directory with `runc`'s incorrectly-timed `prestart` hook trigger (opencontainers/runc#1710).  With this pull request, I extend hooks with a `precreate` stage to allow trusted parties to manipulate the config JSON before calling the runtime's `create`.

I'm recycling the existing `Hook` schema from `pkg/hooks` for this, because we'll want `Timeout` for reliability and `When` to [avoid the expense of fork/exec when a given hook does not need to make config changes][3].

Fixes #1828.

[3]: https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/1828#issuecomment-439888059

comments.body:
I've pushed c565825 -> fafab4b adding logging.  The fafab4b commit message has an example of the generated logs and worries about their not including the container ID.  How strongly do we feel about including the container ID in these logs?  If we want it, do maintainers prefer:

```go
func RuntimeConfigFilter(ctx context.Context, hooks []spec.Hook, config *spec.Spec, postKillTimeout time.Duration, logger *logrus.Entry) (hookErr, err error) {
```

or (with [go-log's `log.Logger` interface][1]):

```go
func RuntimeConfigFilter(ctx context.Context, hooks []spec.Hook, config *spec.Spec, postKillTimeout time.Duration, logger *log.Logger) (hookErr, err error) {
```

Also (again, as mentioned in fafab4b), I was looking at the MIT-licensed testify/assert code when working up this commit.  I think the portions I've copied are small enough to not be copyrightable, but if folks feel otherwise (or just want to be very conservative), let me know and I'll put them in a separate file with a copy of the testify/assert MIT license in the header.

[1]: https://godoc.org/github.com/go-log/log#Logger
